capability_objective: I’m reconciling two records for a handoff. Search separately for “Truck 8 garden delivery” in Depot B and “Sedan 4 clinic courier” in Depot C, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete vehicle records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Return exactly three bullets: one per record in the order requested, then one comparison bullet; include both returned IDs.
prompt:
Reconcile exactly two fleet records in the supplied sandbox for a handoff: `Truck 8 garden delivery` in `Depot B` and `Sedan 4 clinic courier` in `Depot C`. Use Pi's genuine shell tool to run the local `./fleet-registry` executable. Treat the executable as the sole supported interface to fleet records. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing data, its runtime audit directory, or anything under `.protected/`; do not manufacture, replay, or delete operation evidence.

In one Pi shell-tool action, start two separate exact vehicle searches concurrently, one scoped to each requested location and name, and wait for both processes to finish. The searches must be distinct executable invocations launched by the same shell action; do not combine them into a broad search, search for another record, or perform either search in a different action. Continue past this gate only if each exact search independently returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable vehicle ID. If either lookup does not resolve uniquely or its returned record does not exactly match the requested location and name, stop before every retrieval, do not create the handoff file, and identify the unresolved lookup in the final response. Never guess an ID.

Only after both search processes have completed successfully and uniquely, use a later, separate Pi shell-tool action to start two complete-record retrievals concurrently, one distinct executable invocation per vehicle, and wait for both processes to finish. Each retrieval must use only the stable ID returned by its corresponding search. Do not begin either retrieval while a search is running, retrieve a distractor, combine the two IDs into one invocation, or derive status or date from search summaries, backing data, or any other source. Compare status and date only after both complete records have returned.

This task is strictly read-only. Do not update, cancel, notify, create, delete, archive, or otherwise mutate either requested vehicle or any other record. Use no fleet operation beyond the two exact searches and the two eligible complete-record retrievals. Do not alter managed state or execution evidence. Apart from runtime evidence created automatically by the executable, create only `handoff.md`, and leave no temporary coordination artifacts.

When both records resolve, write `handoff.md` as exactly three newline-terminated, nonblank Markdown bullet lines, with no heading, blank lines, or extra prose. Preserve the requested record order. Format each of the first two lines exactly as `- <name> | <location> | ID <stable ID> | status <status> | date <date>`, preserving those five fields exactly as returned by its complete-record retrieval. Format the third line exactly as `- Comparison | statuses <match-or-differ> (<first status> vs <second status>) | dates <match-or-differ> (<first date> vs <second date>)`, replacing each `<match-or-differ>` with `match` only when the corresponding returned values are equal and with `differ` otherwise. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. If it passes, return exactly the three bullet lines as the final response.
